name: John Lennon
abstract: John Winston Ono Lennon (born John Winston Lennon; 9 October 1940 – 8 December 1980) was an English musician, songwriter and activist. He gained global fame as the founder, co-lead vocalist and rhythm guitarist of the Beatles.

works.t: I Want to Hold Your Hand
I Want to Hold Your Hand

works_created.t: Help--All You Need Is Love--Hey Jude
Got to Get You Into My Life
Ticket to Ride--A Hard Day's Night
With a Little Help from My Friends

about_quotes.speaker: Howard Johnson
about_quotes.evidence: I jotted down a few — I think it’s really fascinating that you played on “Escalator Over the Hill,” and then you played with John Lennon. Is that true? [INTERVIEWEE] Yes.
about_quotes.doc_title: Howard Johnson interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 14, 2019

about_quotes.speaker: Laurence Juber
about_quotes.evidence: That was the show where John Lennon made the wise crack about clap along... My 11th birthday was a week later and I got a guitar.
about_quotes.doc_title: Laurence Juber interviewed by Monk Rowe, Glasgow, Scotland, July 27, 2016 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Toots Thielemans
about_quotes.evidence: But I met him in 1962, when the Beatles came for the first time to the States. ... I went to meet him in the Plaza Hotel.
about_quotes.doc_title: Toots Thielemans — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Butch Miles
about_quotes.evidence: And I think John Lennon was right. He said, “All you regular folks just applaud, royalty can just shake their jewelry.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Butch Miles interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, October 9, 2020 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Charles Calello
about_quotes.evidence: as John Lennon once said, “The difference between professionals and amateurs, amateurs copy and professionals steal.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Charles Calello interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, July 21, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Bob Kinkel
about_quotes.evidence: Roy Cicala was the owner of Record Plant. He was John Lennon’s engineer.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bob Kinkel interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, October 31, 2012
